compiled_state.author: Iso Tester
compiled_state.description:
RISK 2 (over-cap growth). `src/rebar/llm/prompts.py` crossed the soft cap this window (745 -> 827) and was ADDED to the allowlist rather than split. Split along the front-matter seam so it drops back off the allowlist.

## What / files — careful split along the front-matter seam
- Extract the front-matter I/O cluster to a NEW `src/rebar/llm/prompts_frontmatter.py` (per `docs/architecture.md:219`): `parse_front_matter`, `_split_front_matter_raw`, `write_front_matter`, `_refuse_newer_schema_version`, plus the constants `FRONT_MATTER_KEYS`, `PROMPT_SCHEMA_VERSION`, the module-private regex `_FRONT_MATTER`, and the exception classes `PromptError`, `PromptVersionError`. `PromptVersionError` subclasses `PromptError`, so both MUST move together (leaving the base behind would make the new module import `prompts.py` and cycle).
- The new module depends ONLY on the stdlib (`re`, `warnings`, lazy `yaml`) + `rebar.llm.errors` (for `LLMConfigError`, the base of `PromptError`). It MUST NOT import `prompts.py`, so there is no import cycle.

## Symbol disposition (explicit, to avoid implementer ambiguity)
- MOVE to `prompts_frontmatter.py`: `parse_front_matter`, `_split_front_matter_raw`, `write_front_matter`, `_refuse_newer_schema_version`, `FRONT_MATTER_KEYS`, `PROMPT_SCHEMA_VERSION`, `_FRONT_MATTER`, `PromptError`, `PromptVersionError`.
- STAY in `prompts.py` (they cannot move — they call `resolve_prompt`/`get_prompt`/`load_prompt` or are only used there): `PromptNotFound` (subclass of the now-imported `PromptError`), `ReviewerError`, `EXECUTION_MODES`, `VOLATILE_MARKER`/`_BASE_MARKER`, the cache-split helpers (`split_volatile`/`strip_volatile_marker`/`resolve_prompt_cached`), `get_prompt`, `load_prompt`, `resolve_prompt`, `prompt_override_drift`, the index/catalog helpers, etc.
- `prompts.py` imports the moved cluster back from `prompts_frontmatter.py` (so its own functions — `get_prompt`, `load_prompt`, `prompt_override_drift` raise/parse via the imported names) AND re-exports them for back-compat.

## Re-export shim (preferred over editing call-sites)
Re-export is preferred over rewriting the ~6 call-sites because several callers use lazy/inline `from rebar.llm.prompts import ...` and updating them would scatter the diff across unrelated modules for no correctness gain. The shim MUST cover EVERY name imported elsewhere from `rebar.llm.prompts`, INCLUDING the private `_split_front_matter_raw` (imported cross-module by `prompt_library.py`). Re-export list: `PromptError`, `PromptVersionError`, `parse_front_matter`, `_split_front_matter_raw`, `write_front_matter`, `_refuse_newer_schema_version`, `FRONT_MATTER_KEYS`, `PROMPT_SCHEMA_VERSION`. F401 is satisfied via an `__all__` entry (the repo's facade convention).

## External import sites that must keep working (verified by grep)
`src/rebar/llm/prompt_library.py` (`PromptError`, `_split_front_matter_raw`, `write_front_matter`); `src/rebar/llm/workflow/editor.py` (`parse_front_matter`, `write_front_matter`, `PromptError`); `src/rebar/llm/workflow/prompt_authoring.py` (`PromptError`, `write_front_matter`); `src/rebar/llm/workflow/runs.py` (`PromptError`); `tests/unit/test_prompt_front_matter.py` (`FRONT_MATTER_KEYS`, `PROMPT_SCHEMA_VERSION`, `PromptError`, `PromptVersionError`, `_split_front_matter_raw`, `parse_front_matter`, `write_front_matter`); `tests/unit/test_execution_mode.py` (`PromptError`). All keep importing from `rebar.llm.prompts` unchanged.

## Docs to update (no stale references after the split)
- `docs/architecture.md` (~line 219): update the `llm/prompts.py` offenders-table remedy row to state the front-matter I/O now lives in `prompts_frontmatter.py` and `prompts.py` re-exports it, and drop `prompts.py` from the "over-cap" set. Remove `src/rebar/llm/prompts.py` from `.github/module-size-allowlist.txt`.
- Grep the tree for any other reference describing `prompts.py` as holding the front-matter cluster and fix it.

## Rollback / expand-contract sequencing (reversible)
1. Create `prompts_frontmatter.py` with the cluster and add the re-export imports to `prompts.py` WITHOUT yet deleting the originals; run the full suite. 2. Then remove the original definitions from `prompts.py` (now provided by the re-exports); run the suite again. If step 2 surfaces a runtime ImportError, revert ONLY the deletion — `prompts.py` with re-exports still behaves as before. The whole change is one commit, revertible wholesale.

## Scope
In: front-matter cluster extraction + re-export shim + the doc/allowlist updates above. Out: changing the prompt schema or resolution logic (byte-for-byte behavior preserved).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `prompts_frontmatter.py` created (>=100 LOC) holding `parse_front_matter`, `_split_front_matter_raw`, `write_front_matter`, `_refuse_newer_schema_version`, `FRONT_MATTER_KEYS`, `PROMPT_SCHEMA_VERSION`, `_FRONT_MATTER`, `PromptError`, `PromptVersionError` — and NONE of these definitions remain in `prompts.py` (only re-export aliases).
- [ ] `prompts.py` drops BELOW 800 LOC and is REMOVED from `.github/module-size-allowlist.txt`.
- [ ] Public AND cross-module-private symbols (incl. `_split_front_matter_raw`) remain importable from `prompts.py` (re-export) — every external import site above still imports unchanged; no caller breakage.
- [ ] `prompts_frontmatter.py` does not import `prompts.py` (no import cycle); `prompts.py` imports the cluster from it.
- [ ] `docs/architecture.md` offenders table updated to the post-split two-file layout; no stale reference to `prompts.py` holding the front-matter cluster survives a grep.
- [ ] The prompt front-matter / drift-gate tests pass (`make lint` + `make test`).
compiled_state.title: Risk 2: split prompts.py front-matter cluster (drop off allowlist)
